stem: The function \( u(x,y) = ([[a]])\left(x^{3} - 3xy^{2}\right) \) is harmonic. Let \( v \) be a harmonic conjugate of \( u \), so that \( f = u + iv \) is analytic. By the Cauchy-Riemann equations \( v_{y} = u_{x} \). Evaluate \( v_{y} \) at the point \( ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \).
solution: By Cauchy-Riemann, \( v_{y} = u_{x} = ([[a]])(3x^{2} - 3y^{2}) \). At \( ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \) this equals \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( u_{y} = -6axy \) evaluated at the point; the Cauchy-Riemann relation needed here is \( v_{y}=u_{x} \), not \( u_{y} \).
This uses \( x^{2}+y^{2} \) instead of \( x^{2}-y^{2} \) (sign error in \( u_{x} \)).
This drops the factor of 3 from differentiating the cubic terms.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The function \( u(x,y) = ([[a]])\left(x^{3} - 3xy^{2}\right) \) is harmonic, and \( v \) is a harmonic conjugate of \( u \), so \( f = u + iv \) is analytic. By the Cauchy-Riemann equations \( v_{x} = -u_{y} \). Evaluate \( v_{x} \) at the point \( ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \).
solution: By Cauchy-Riemann, \( v_{x} = -u_{y} \). Since \( u_{y} = -6([[a]])xy \), we get \( v_{x} = 6([[a]])xy \); at \( ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \) this equals \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( u_{x} \) evaluated at the point, not \( v_{x} = -u_{y} \).
This is off by a sign; \( v_x = -u_y \), and the negative signs of \( u_y \) were double-counted incorrectly.
This has the wrong overall sign relative to the correct Cauchy-Riemann relation.
This negates the correct value (a further sign slip).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For the harmonic function \( u(x,y) = ([[a]])\left(x^{3} - 3xy^{2}\right) \), compute the second partial derivative \( u_{xx} \) at the point \( ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \).
solution: \( u_{x} = ([[a]])(3x^{2}-3y^{2}) \), so \( u_{xx} = 6([[a]])x \). At \( x=[[x0]] \) this equals \( [[answer]] \) (and one can check \( u_{yy} = -6([[a]])x = -[[answer]] \), confirming \( u_{xx}+u_{yy}=0 \), i.e. \( u \) is harmonic).
distractor_rationales: This is \( u_{yy} \), the negative of \( u_{xx} \), not \( u_{xx} \) itself.
This stops at \( u_{x} \) without differentiating a second time.
This mixes up the roles of \( x \) and \( y \) in the second derivative.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: The function \( u(x,y) = ([[a]])\left(x^{3} - 3xy^{2}\right) \) is harmonic. Its harmonic conjugate \( v \) satisfying \( v(0,0)=0 \) is \( v(x,y) = ([[a]])\left(3x^{2}y - y^{3}\right) \). Evaluate \( v([[x0]], [[y0]]) \).
solution: Substituting the point directly into \( v(x,y) = ([[a]])(3x^{2}y - y^{3}) \) gives \( v([[x0]],[[y0]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( u(x_0,y_0) \) instead of \( v(x_0,y_0) \).
This has the wrong sign on both terms of \( v \).
This drops the \( -y^{3} \) term of \( v \) entirely.
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
